case_title: KAITLYN JOHNSON ET AL VS. KATHLEEN MCHUGH ET AL
cause_of_action: PETITION RE: ARBITRATION (PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD)

docket_entries.description: NOTICE OF MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRTION AWARD; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT THEREOF (TRANSACTION ID # 74608956) FILED BY PETITIONER JOHNSON, KAITLYN GASPARINI, CAROLINE HEARING SET FOR OCT-23-2024 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 501
SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY, (TRANSACTION ID # 74716068): RASKIN, ELLIS F. SUBSTITUTED FOR EMBLIDGE, GEORGE SCOTT AS ATTORNEY FOR MCHUGH, KATHLEEN
PROOF OF SERVICE BY ELECTRONIC MAIL (BY E-MAIL OR ELECTRONIC TRANSMISSION) (TRANSACTION ID # 74716068) FILED BY RESPONDENT MCHUGH, KATHLEEN BLACK, ASHLEY

DECLARATION OF KAITLYN JOHNSON IN SUPPORT OF PETITIONERS REPLY TO RESPONDENTS OPPOSITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD (TRANSACTION ID # 74778265) FILED BY PETITIONER JOHNSON, KAITLYN GASPARINI, CAROLINE

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PETITIONER KAITLYN JOHNSON, CAROLINE GASPARINI NOTICE OF MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRTION AWARD; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT THEREOF IS GRANTED. THE AWARD IS CONFIRMED AS STATED IN THE FINAL AWARD. RESPONDENT FIRST APPEARED IN THIS ACTION ON JUNE 27, 2024 STATING THAT "THIS COURT SHOULD CONFIRM THE FINAL AWARD AS IS," OBJECTING ONLY TO ADDITIONAL LANGUAGE REQUESTED BY THE PETITIONER. THE PROPOSED ADDITIONAL LANGUAGE SHALL NOT BE INCLUDED IN THE JUDGMENT AND AWARD IS CONFIRMED AS IS. IN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO CONFIRM FILED ON OCTOBER 9, 2024 (LONG AFTER THE 100-DAY CCP 1288.2 DEADLINE HAS PASSED) RESPONDENT FOR THE FIRST TIME IN THIS ACTION CLAIMS THAT (1) NO PART OF THE AWARD SHOULD BE CONFIRMED BECAUSE PETITIONER ASKED FOR ADDITIONAL LANGUAGE; NO AUTHORITY IS CITED FOR THIS PROPOSITION; (2) THE AWARD SHOULD BE MODIFIED; NO ARGUMENT PROVIDED AS TO THE REQUESTED MODIFICATION; AND (3) THE AWARD SHOULD BE VACATED PER CCP 1286.2(A)(5) DUE TO THE ARBITRATOR'S REFUSAL TO HEAR EVIDENCE MATERIAL TO THE CONTROVERSY. ON THE LAST POINT RESPONDENT PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE THAT ARBITRATOR "REFUSED" TO CONSIDER ANY EVIDENCE PROPERLY PRESENTED TO HIM. RESPONDENTS ALSO CLAIM ENTITLEMENT TO EQUITABLE TOLLING AND EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL RE: 100-DAY DEADLINE TO FILE A REQUEST TO VACATE OR CORRECT THE AWARD. RESPONDENTS PRESENT NO EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT EACH ELEMENT OF THESE EQUITABLE RELIEF THEORIES AS IT RELATES TO RESPONDENTS' FAILURE TO SEEK TO VACATE/MODIFY THE AWARD WITHIN 100 DAYS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF MOTION TO ENFORCE JUDGMENT, ORDER CIVIL CONTEMPT AND/OR APPOINT A RECEIVER IS GRANTED, IN PART. THE DEODAR CEDAR TREE IN THE COMMON AREA OF THE HOA, WHICH WAS THE SUBJECT OF THE ARBITRATION AWARD AND JUDGMENT ENTERED IN THIS MATTER ON OCTOBER 23, 2024 SHALL BE REMOVED NO LATER THAN MAY 9, 2025. COSTS FOR THE REMOVAL OF THE SUBJECT TREE SHALL BE BORNE BY THE NOMINAL RESPONDENT, THE 217 / 219 DIVISADERO STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION AND/OR SPLIT EQUALLY BETWEEN PETITIONERS AND RESPONDENTS. A COMPLIANCE HEARING SHALL BE SET IN THIS MATTER FOR MAY 16, 2025. IF THE SUBJECT DEODAR CEDAR TREE IN THE COMMON AREA OF THE HOA IS NOT REMOVED BY MAY 9, 2025, THEN AT THE MAY 16, 2026 COMPLIANCE HEARING THE COURT SHALL HOLD A HEARING TO DETERMINE WHETHER PATRICK J. CONNOLLY, ESQ. SHALL BE APPOINTED A RECEIVER TO CARRY OUT THE TERMS OF THE JUDGMENT, TO WIT: REMOVE THE SUBJECT TREE. RESPONDENT'S OBJECTION TO EXHIBITS 6 AND 8 OF THE LIGHTFOOT DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. PETITIONER'S REQUEST TO SET A HEARING FOR CIVIL CONTEMPT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Enforce Judgment, Order Civil Contempt And/Or Appoint A Receiver
Off Calendar Apr-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Stephen Lightfoot; 415-945-9998; Lightfoot Law Firm; counsel for petitioner appearing via Zoom.
Ellis Raskin; 916-442-9999; Hanson Bridgett; appearing as counsel for respondent.

Mr. Raskin contests the courts tentative ruling. Argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER KAITLYN JOHNSON, CAROLINE GASPARINI Notice Of Motion To Enforce Judgment, Order Civil Contempt And/Or Appoint A Receiver is GRANTED, in part. The deodar cedar tree in the Common Area of the HOA, which was the subject of the Arbitration Award and Judgment entered in this matter on October 23, 2024 shall be removed no later than May 9, 2025. Costs for the removal of the subject tree shall be borne by the nominal Respondent, the 217 / 219 Divisadero Street Homeowners Association and/or split equally between Petitioners and Respondents. A compliance hearing shall be set in this matter for May 16, 2025. If the subject deodar cedar tree in the Common Area of the HOA is not removed by May 9, 2025, then at the May 16, 2026 compliance hearing the Court shall hold a hearing to determine whether Patrick J. Connolly, Esq. shall be appointed a Receiver to carry out the terms of the Judgment, to wit: remove the subject tree. Respondent's objection to Exhibits 6 and 8 of the Lightfoot Declaration is overruled. Petitioner's request to set a hearing for civil contempt is denied without prejudice.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion To Confirm Arbitrtion Award; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-23-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Stephen Lightfoot Esq., Lightfoot Firm, 415-945-9998, in-perso appearance for petitioner
Ellis Raskin Esq., Hanson Bridgett LLP, 415-777-3200, via zoom for respondent

Mr. Raskin contests to the tentative. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER KAITLYN JOHNSON, CAROLINE GASPARINI NOTICE OF MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRTION AWARD; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT THEREOF is GRANTED. The award is confirmed as stated in the Final Award. Respondent first appeared in this action on June 27, 2024 stating that "this Court should confirm the Final Award as is," objecting only to additional language requested by the Petitioner. The proposed additional language shall not be included in the judgment and award is confirmed as is.
In opposition to the Motion to Confirm filed on October 9, 2024 (long after the 100-day CCP 1288.2 deadline has passed) Respondent for the first time in this action claims that (1) no part of the award should be confirmed because Petitioner asked for additional language; no authority is cited for this proposition; (2) the award should be modified; no argument provided as to the requested modification; and (3) the award should be vacated per CCP 1286.2(a)(5) due to the Arbitrator's refusal to hear evidence material to the controversy. On the last point Respondent presents no evidence that Arbitrator "refused" to consider any evidence properly presented to him. Respondents also claim entitlement to equitable tolling and equitable estoppel re: 100-day deadline to file a request to vacate or correct the award. Respondents present no evidence to support each element of these equitable relief theories as it relates to Respondents' failure to seek to vacate/modify the award within 100 days.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
